_readme: One entry per third-party integration. 'ourEndpoint' = the route on OUR backend that triggers the call (or receives their webhook). 'theirEndpoint' = the real third-party REST endpoint we hit. 'sampleRequest'/'sampleResponse' marked (live-captured) are REAL payloads captured this session via logs/<service>-*.log or a direct safe test call; everything else is (constructed) — built from the exact field names our own code reads/writes, not guessed. 'used: false' entries are real endpoints this provider offers that our code does NOT call today — candidates for 'better handling' work.
generatedAt: 2026-07-22 (refreshed 2026-07-23: razorpay fee/tax/method persistence + real order data, mailer switched SMTP→Resend, auth ipapi.co rate-limit finding, shadowfax real production data + dead-serviceability-check gap, delhivery every endpoint now explicitly marked liveTested:true/false; refreshed 2026-07-24: fixed a real status-label/rank bug in shadowfax.js found by actually testing every official status_id, removed the unconfirmed 'live tracking link' from admin UI, re-confirmed staging never progresses past 'new' with fresh 24h+ evidence)

razorpay.mode: TEST (rzp_test_… key — no real money moves)
razorpay.realOrderUsedThroughout: ADC20260722104807 (order id 63) — a genuine order you placed and paid for during this session. Every 'liveCaptured' sample below is the actual request/response for THIS real order, pulled straight from logs/razorpay-2026-07-22.log — not synthetic test data.
razorpay.feeTaxMethodNowPersisted: Added 2026-07-23: payments table gained razorpay_fee, razorpay_tax, method, card_network, card_last4, vpa, bank, amount_refunded columns. finalizePaidOrder() now pulls these straight out of the Razorpay payment object (already fetched for the status check above) on every new payment — no extra API call needed. NOT yet live-verified in our own DB — order 68/ADC20260722211553/pay_TGh3KrrOHwBks7 (fee=₹3.60, tax=₹0, method=card debit, network=Visa, last4=1111, seen on the Razorpay dashboard) was paid BEFORE this migration+code landed, so its payments row still has these columns NULL. Will confirm with the next real order placed after this deploys.

endpoints.purpose: Create the Razorpay Order the Checkout popup attaches the payment to (best practice #2: Integrate Orders API)
endpoints.note: receipt is always our own order_number (e.g. ADC20260722104807), never a made-up value — this is what ties Razorpay's order back to ours.

endpoints.purpose: Verify the signature Checkout hands back after payment — proves the payment is genuine WITHOUT a second network call (best practice #3: Verify Signature)
endpoints.theirEndpoint: (no call — local HMAC-SHA256 math: HMAC(order_id|payment_id, key_secret) must equal razorpay_signature)
endpoints.note: orderId/paymentId here are the real Razorpay IDs for order ADC20260722104807 — same order as the row above.

endpoints.purpose: Fetch a payment's actual server-side status/amount directly, instead of only trusting the signature (best practice #4: Check Payment/Order Status Before Providing Services). Added 2026-07-22 — the order/amount must match exactly and status must be 'captured' before we mark PAID.
endpoints.ourEndpoint: internal — called from POST /api/orders/:id/payment/verify and POST /api/payment-callback/:orderId, right after the signature check passes

verifiedNegativeCase_liveCaptured.test: same signature check made to pass with a made-up payment id (pay_FAKENONEXISTENT123), to prove this step actually blocks a forged/garbage payment id even when the signature is valid

error.description: The id provided does not exist

verifiedNegativeCase_liveCaptured.outcome: our /payment/verify correctly returned 502 and the order stayed PENDING — nothing marked paid

endpoints.purpose: Razorpay's redirect callback for in-app browsers that can't run the iframe/popup flow — Instagram/FB Messenger, Opera Mini, UC Browser (best practice #6: Use Callback URL). Added 2026-07-22.
endpoints.ourEndpoint: POST /api/payment-callback/:orderId?return=<our own origin>  (public — no auth header, since this is a browser form POST from Razorpay's checkout page, not a server-to-server call)
endpoints.theirEndpoint: (inbound — a browser form submission from Razorpay's hosted Checkout page, not an S2S call, so it works even on localhost without a tunnel)
endpoints.sameChecksAsVerify: signature check, then the same fetchPayment status/amount confirmation as above, before marking PAID
testedLive_allPassed: untrusted return= origin (e.g. an attacker's domain) → rejected, never redirects off our own domain (open-redirect guard)

endpoints.purpose: Inbound webhook — server-to-server confirmation of payment/order/refund/dispute events (backstop for the browser handler, and the ONLY way we ever learn about failures/refunds/disputes) (best practice #5: Use Webhooks)
endpoints.ourEndpoint: POST /api/payments/webhook  (raw body, X-Razorpay-Signature header verified against RAZORPAY_WEBHOOK_SECRET)
endpoints.theirEndpoint: (inbound — Razorpay calls US; configured in Razorpay Dashboard → Settings → Webhooks)
realFailedPaymentsOnDashboard_forReferen: pay_TEIb6TkajTX42P — ₹340, Gateway – Technical Error, Fri Jul 17 1:32am
pay_TEIaHWc8cWhsQY — ₹340, Gateway – Technical Error, Fri Jul 17 1:32am
endpoints.note: The payment ids in this sample are your two REAL test-mode gateway failures (from the Razorpay dashboard screenshot). This exact webhook body was never actually sent by Razorpay for those two (webhook handling for payment.failed didn't exist yet when they happened) — it's reconstructed with your real payment/order ids so the shape is genuine even though Razorpay didn't fire it live. The order_tracking write logic itself WAS proven live (see below).

endpoints.purpose: Issue a refund on a captured payment (added 2026-07-22, not yet exercised with a real TEST-mode refund — only its request/response wiring + the confirmation webhook handling below are proven)
endpoints.ourEndpoint: not yet wired to an admin UI button — the function createRefund() exists in src/razorpay.js and is ready to call, but nothing in admin.js invokes it yet
endpoints.whyNotFullyLiveTested: Would create a real (TEST-mode) refund against your real order ADC20260722104807 — held off on actually calling it without asking first, since it permanently marks that real order's payment status. Say the word and I'll run it for genuinely live end-to-end data.

confirmedWorking_viaWebhook.test: sent a properly-signed refund.processed event (real order ADC20260722104807 / pay_TGQjlDWBr9vzHl) to prove the RECEIVING side works
confirmedWorking_viaWebhook.result_liveCaptured: order_tracking row inserted: status=REFUNDED, remarks='Refund re_TESTREFUND01 — ₹130.00 — refund.processed'; payments.status updated to REFUNDED — then reverted after the test since no real refund happened

endpoints.purpose: Chargeback / dispute notification (added 2026-07-22)
endpoints.ourEndpoint: handled inside POST /api/payments/webhook for event payment.dispute.created
endpoints.theirEndpointNote: arrives only as a webhook event, no separate REST call to make

confirmedWorking_viaWebhook.result_liveCaptured: order_tracking row inserted: status=DISPUTE_OPENED, remarks='⚠ Chargeback/dispute opened — ₹130.00 — reason=goods_not_received — respond by 2026-07-29T18:35:21.000Z' — then reverted after the test since no real dispute happened

endpoints.purpose: List all payments against one Order — catches a duplicate CAPTURED charge from a retried payment (best practice #4's third check, alongside Fetch Payment above). Added 2026-07-23.
endpoints.ourEndpoint: internal — checkForDuplicateCharge() in src/routes/orders.js, called right after finalizePaidOrder() in both POST /api/orders/:id/payment/verify and POST /api/payment-callback/:orderId
endpoints.note: Real result for ADC20260722104807: exactly 1 captured payment (count:1) — no duplicate. If count of captured items were ever >1, we insert an order_tracking row (status=DUPLICATE_CHARGE_WARNING) and it now shows as a red '⚠ Possible duplicate charge' badge directly in Admin → Orders (both the list table and the order detail modal) — never auto-refunds, just surfaces it for a human to check in the Razorpay dashboard.

delhivery.docs: Delhivery One Panel → Settings → API Setup / B2C API docs
delhivery.mode: staging (https://staging-express.delhivery.com) — used as the outstation/inter-city carrier fallback when a pincode isn't in a Shadowfax store zone
delhivery.dbCheck_2026-07-23: 6 real orders with carrier=DELHIVERY, all label_generated=true, all consistent — no bad data found. Unlike Shadowfax, checkServiceability()/expectedTat() ARE genuinely wired into the /api/delivery/check checkout route (confirmed by reading delivery.js) — no dead-code gap here; the checkout-time promise is real.
delhivery.liveTestedSummary_2026-07-23_SUPERSEDED: Original note (2026-07-23): only 4 of 10 endpoints were proven live. SUPERSEDED 2026-07-24 — see liveTestedSummary_2026-07-24 below for the current, much more complete picture after switching to real production credentials and testing every endpoint directly.

liveTestedSummary_2026-07-24.productionSwitchConfirmed: DELHIVERY_BASE_URL switched from staging to production (https://track.delhivery.com), DELIVERY_API_TOKEN replaced with a real production token. Confirmed via Delhivery's own docs for 2 endpoints (Serviceability, Document Download) before switching — not assumed.
confirmedWorkingOnRealProduction: Pickup request booking (mechanics confirmed; see note on that endpoint — no clean success response observed yet)
Shipping label PDF (full real PDF verified, %PDF-1.7 header)
Fetch document (confirmed real expected-rejection behavior for an undelivered shipment)
confirmedBrokenOnRealProduction: Cancel shipment — genuine, reproducible failure, see that endpoint's entry for full detail. Tried 4 different request-body variants, all fail identically, even on a completely fresh never-touched shipment. Not a guess, not a one-off — a real, confirmed gap.

realTestArtifacts_2026-07-24.testWarehouse: ADC-Test-Satya-Pickup — Satya's real address (24-18-03, Green Fields Layout, Mullur, 560035), registered + edited successfully on real production Delhivery, client code 26f42e-ADC-do confirmed.
realTestArtifacts_2026-07-24.prepaidTestOrder: waybill 57064410000125 — Satya → Surya (Srinivasa Lavish PG, Gachibowli, Hyderabad, 500032), ₹60 Chocolate Chip Cookie. Real progression observed: Manifested → Pickup scheduled → Not Picked (after manual portal cancellation) — the first time ANY carrier's shipment has shown real status progression beyond initial creation this whole project.
realTestArtifacts_2026-07-24.codTestOrder: waybill 57064410000136 — same route, payment_mode='COD' instead of 'Prepaid', cod_amount=60. Created specifically to test whether COD bypasses Delhivery's ₹500 minimum-wallet-balance requirement for pickup requests — confirmed it does (see pickup-request endpoint entry). This is a TEST-ONLY technique — ADC's real orders stay prepaid-only, COD was never used for an actual customer order.

liveTestedSummary_2026-07-24.walletBalanceFinding: Delhivery's real account wallet showed ₹442.04 at test time — booking a pickup request for a Prepaid shipment via the API requires ≥₹500 and was rejected with a real error: {"prepaid":"Client wallet balance is 442.04 which is less than 500.0"}. Confirmed real, not guessed. Booking the SAME pickup manually through the One Panel portal succeeded anyway (no balance complaint) — so the ₹500 check may only apply to Prepaid shipments specifically, or to the API path specifically, not universally to all pickup-booking. Not fully resolved — flagging as a nuance, not a contradiction we've explained.

delhivery.statusCodeCheck_2026-07-24: Hit the real live-tracking endpoint directly on 3 real waybills (85582010000313, 85582010000302, 85582010000280) — actually calling it, same rigor as the Shadowfax status-code test. All 3 show status='Manifested'. Waybill 85582010000313 (order 57, created 2026-07-17) is STILL 'Manifested' a full WEEK later, with Instructions='Pickup not attempted' — this is strong, repeated evidence that Delhivery's staging sandbox ALSO doesn't self-progress orders through pickup/transit/delivery, same finding as Shadowfax, just with even longer elapsed time. No architectural bug on the Delhivery side (unlike Shadowfax): Delhivery has NO webhook at all in this codebase — tracking only ever comes from polling, so there's no push-vs-pull race for a rank system to protect against.
bugsFoundAndFixed_2026-07-24: Customer-facing track route (routes/orders.js) was showing Delhivery's bare Status.Status word ('Manifested') and silently discarding the much more informative Status.Instructions field ('Pickup not attempted') that's sitting right next to it in the same real response. Fixed to join both — real data joined together, nothing invented.

Admin's Shadowfax track route (routes/admin.js /orders/:id/track) was writing the RAW status_id (e.g. 'new') straight into orders.shipment_status, while the customer-facing route (routes/orders.js) correctly writes the friendly label (sfxStatusLabel(result.status), e.g. 'Order placed') into the exact same column. Depending on which route ran last, the same field held two different formats. Fixed admin's route to use sfxStatusLabel() too, matching the customer route.
Admin's Delhivery track route had the same missing-Instructions gap as the customer route above — fixed identically.

endpoints.purpose: Check whether a destination pincode is serviceable + COD/prepaid support
endpoints.realCallConfirmed_viaRailwayLogs: 2026-07-23T05:21:50Z — pincode 523226 → ✓ serviceable, cod=true (fresh, real production traffic, not a synthetic test)
endpoints.productionBaseUrlConfirmed_2026-07-24: DELHIVERY_BASE_URL switched from staging (https://staging-express.delhivery.com) to production (https://track.delhivery.com) — confirmed correct via Delhivery's own official docs for 2 separate endpoints (this one, and the Document Download API), not assumed. Tested locally with the new production DELIVERY_API_TOKEN against 3 real pincodes: 560067 (Jaya's), 560035 (Satya's), 560041 (our Jayanagar store) — all serviceable=true, embargo=false. Also tested 110001 (New Delhi) as a sanity check — came back embargo=true (a real Delhivery-side regional restriction, not a bug). Unlike Shadowfax, no evidence yet of a split production domain across endpoints, but not fully verified for every one — watch for mismatches as each is tested.

endpoints.purpose: Expected delivery date / transit time (TAT) between two pincodes
endpoints.realCallConfirmed_viaRailwayLogs: 2026-07-23T05:21:50Z — 560041→523226 → ✓ tat=3 days (same real production traffic as the serviceability check above)

endpoints.purpose: Pre-assign a waybill (AWB) number before creating a shipment
endpoints.ourEndpoint: internal — called from order auto-shipment flow (POST /api/orders/:id/… ) and admin manual shipment create

endpoints.purpose: Register a pickup warehouse (name must exactly match pickup_location used at shipment creation)
endpoints.ourEndpoint: POST /api/admin/warehouses (admin creates/edits a store's pickup location)

sampleRequest_constructed.name: A Dough Cookie — Jayanagar
sampleRequest_constructed.address: Jain University, 1314, 24th Main Rd, Jayanagar 9th Block

endpoints.realCallConfirmed_2026-07-24: Registered a real test warehouse on PRODUCTION Delhivery (name='ADC-Test-Satya-Pickup', Satya's address, 560035) — succeeded, status 201: {message: 'A new client warehouse has been created in HQ(Delhivery).', active: true, client: '26f42e-ADC-do'}. Confirms our real Delhivery client account code and that this endpoint works cleanly (unlike Shadowfax's equivalent, no observed display bugs). Real client business_hours defaulted to Mon-Sat 09:30-18:30. Confirmed showing up correctly and immediately in Delhivery's own One Panel dashboard (Settings > Pickup Locations) — no mismatch between API and portal.
endpoints.editAlsoLiveTested_2026-07-24: updateWarehouseOnDelhivery() (the /edit/ endpoint) also confirmed working: edited ADC-Test-Satya-Pickup to add an address_line2 ('Near Mullur Junction') — succeeded, real response: {message: 'client warehouse has been updated in HQ(Delhivery).', address: '24-18-03, Green Fields Layout, Mullur, Near Mullur Junction'}. Clean, no issues.
endpoints.realStoreCheck_2026-07-24: Confirmed via direct DB query: our warehouses table has exactly ONE registered store, 'Adough Cookie' (id 2, pickup_location='Adough Cookie', pincode 560041, is_default=true) — matches Delhivery's One Panel dashboard's pre-existing 'Adough Cookie' entry exactly (same name, city, state). Delhivery only ever uses this single default warehouse for all orders (unlike Shadowfax's per-store routing) since Delhivery is only the outstation/inter-city fallback carrier — no per-store granularity needed here.
endpoints.note: This specific test warehouse (ADC-Test-Satya-Pickup) is separate from the real 'Adough Cookie' warehouse used for actual orders — created purely for today's live testing.

endpoints.purpose: Estimate shipping cost for a given weight/route (admin-only cost calculator)
endpoints.note: Backend route still live, but the admin UI panel for this was removed earlier this project — orphaned-but-functional endpoint
endpoints.realCallConfirmed_2026-07-24: Tested live, 560035→500032, 0.1kg, Prepaid: ✓ total=₹57.96, zone=C1, real tax breakdown (SGST ₹4.42, CGST ₹4.42), real charge line items (charge_DL=44, charge_DPH=1.62, charge_LM=2.5, charge_PEAK=1).

endpoints.purpose: Create the actual outstation shipment (assigns final waybill, sort code)
endpoints.ourEndpoint: internal — order auto-shipment flow after payment, and admin manual create (POST /api/admin/orders/:id/shipment)
endpoints.note: Real responses came back with sort=null on the 2026-07-22 staging-era orders, but real PRODUCTION orders (2026-07-24) correctly return a real sort code (e.g. 'HYD/MGW' for Hyderabad) — staging just didn't bother computing one.

endpoints.purpose: Cancel a shipment
endpoints.liveTestedResult: CONFIRMED BROKEN — genuine, reproducible failure on real production, not a guess or one-off.
endpoints.realBugFound_2026-07-24: Every attempt returns HTTP 200 with a business-level failure: {"error":"Enter Waybill/OrderID, please try again","status":"Failure","waybill":null,"order_id":null} — the waybill is echoed back as null/empty every time, meaning Delhivery isn't receiving our value at all. Tried 4 variants: (1) our existing code's shape {shipments:[{waybill,cancellation:'true'}]}, (2) top-level {waybill,cancellation:'true'}, (3) same as (1) via URLSearchParams instead of manual encodeURIComponent, (4) {waybills:['...'],cancellation:true} plural array. ALL FOUR failed identically. Also tested on a completely fresh, never-touched shipment (ruling out 'already cancelled, can't re-cancel' as the explanation) — same failure. Genuinely unresolved — needs Delhivery's own docs/support to clarify the real expected field name/shape, same situation as several Shadowfax endpoints earlier. The Delhivery MCP server (added to this project's Claude Code config 2026-07-24, connects on next session restart) is the natural next place to resolve this precisely instead of more guessing.
endpoints.note: The one endpoint out of everything tested today that's confirmed NOT working. Do not rely on programmatic cancellation until this is resolved — cancel via the One Panel portal manually in the meantime.

endpoints.purpose: Book a courier pickup request at a warehouse
realCallsConfirmed_2026-07-24: Prepaid shipment, wallet balance ₹442.04 (below Delhivery's ₹500 minimum for Prepaid pickup requests) → real rejection: {"prepaid":"Client wallet balance is 442.04 which is less than 500.0"}

COD shipment, same wallet balance → HTTP 201, but business response was pr_exist:true, error code 669, 'A Pickup Request 307835274 for this Pickup Location Already Exist for 25 Jul in slot 14:00 - 18:00' — confirms COD bypasses the wallet-balance check entirely, but this specific call didn't get a clean fresh success either (hit a duplicate-request conflict instead, since a pickup was already scheduled for that location/date/slot from the earlier manual booking)
endpoints.note: The API mechanics are confirmed to work (real requests, real responses, real business-rule enforcement) — but we have NOT yet observed a genuinely clean 'fresh pickup request created successfully' response from THIS endpoint specifically; every attempt hit either the balance check or a duplicate-slot conflict. A pickup WAS successfully booked, but via the One Panel portal UI, not this API. Worth one more clean test (fresh warehouse/date/slot combo, wallet topped up) before fully trusting this for production use.

endpoints.purpose: Get a printable shipping label PDF (pre-signed URL)
endpoints.realCallConfirmed_2026-07-24: Confirmed the FULL real flow end to end: the endpoint returns JSON (not raw PDF bytes) — {"packages":[{"pdf_download_link":"https://express-hq-prod.s3..."}]} — our admin.js route already correctly anticipated this exact case (comment: 'packing_slip returns EITHER raw PDF bytes OR JSON with a pre-signed pdf_download_link'). Fetched the extracted S3 link directly — got a genuine PDF back, verified by checking the file starts with the real PDF magic bytes '%PDF-1.7', 161KB. Nothing needed fixing here, code was already correct.

endpoints.purpose: Live tracking / current status of a shipment
endpoints.realCallConfirmed_2026-07-24: Tested on 2 real production waybills. Genuinely useful finding: Consignee.Name comes through correctly ('Surya'), unlike Telephone1/2 which are masked blank (privacy) — so name masking behaves differently from Shadowfax's tracking API, which blanks name AND contact both. Also confirmed real status progression for the first time on any carrier this project: Manifested → 'Pickup scheduled' → 'Not Picked' (after the pickup was cancelled) — proves Delhivery's real tracking genuinely reflects real-world events, not stuck like the staging sandbox was.

endpoints.purpose: Fetch delivery proof documents — signature, EPOD, return QC images
endpoints.realCallConfirmed_2026-07-24: Tested EPOD on an undelivered real shipment — got the correctly expected real rejection: HTTP 404, {"success":false,"message":"doc_type: EPOD, is not available for this waybill.","data":{}}. Confirms the endpoint and our error-handling both work correctly for the 'not available yet' case. Have not yet seen the SUCCESS case (would need a real delivered/RTS shipment).

shadowfax.docs: Shadowfax's official Apiary-hosted API docs (full reference read 2026-07-23, cross-checked against our code — see findings below); also adc-cookies-backend-node/documentations/shadowfax.md
shadowfax.mode: staging (https://dale.staging.shadowfax.in/api) — used as the INTRACITY/same-day carrier for orders delivering to a city where we have a store (Bengaluru, Chennai)
shadowfax.prepaidOnly: cod_amount is always '0' / payment_mode 'Prepaid' — ADC never accepts COD

officialStagingTestPincodes_confirmedLiv.note: Shadowfax's own docs list these 3 as sample serviceable pincodes for testing order creation on staging. Confirmed directly against the real serviceability API (not assumed from docs) — all 3 genuinely serviceable right now.

ourRealStorePincodes_serviceabilityConfi.note: Checked our 3 Bengaluru store pincodes (the ones zoneStores()/nearestStore() in src/shadowfax.js actually route pickups to) against the same live API. Now also surfaced in Admin → Delivery → Shadowfax tab as a 'pickup stores' panel, dulled/greyed out when not serviceable, live-checked on every page load (not cached).

shadowfax.dbCheck_2026-07-23: Queried all 9 orders with carrier=SHADOWFAX — all consistent (waybill + tracking_url correctly populated, no nulls where data is expected). One universal-but-harmless gap found, NOT Shadowfax-specific: delhivery_waybill/tracking_url are set correctly on every order regardless of carrier, but delhivery_shipment_id and estimated_delivery are NULL on 100% of orders (checked both SHADOWFAX and DELHIVERY carriers) — the shipment-creation code never writes to either column for any carrier. Not corrupted data, just two schema columns nothing currently populates.
shadowfax.invoiceGeneration_answeredDirectly_2026-: NO — read Shadowfax's complete official API reference (Serviceability, AWB Generation, Order Creation x2, Order Details, Update Order, Order Cancellation, Escalation, POD, QR Code) end to end: there is no invoice-generation or invoice-download endpoint anywhere in their API. The closest thing is 'Generate QR Code' (below) — but that only creates a payment QR code referencing an invoice number/GSTIN/amount YOU already have, it doesn't generate the invoice document itself, and it's explicitly COD-only ('QR Code can only be generated for COD') so it can never apply to ADC anyway (prepaid-only). Confirms what was already suspected earlier this project, now backed by the full official docs rather than a partial read.

warehouseModelConfirmedLive_2026-07-24.summary: ADC uses the Warehouse model, not Marketplace — confirmed correct because ADC owns all 4 stores outright (Marketplace model is for platforms coordinating pickups from independent third-party sellers, which doesn't apply). Same endpoint as marketplace (POST /v3/clients/orders/), differentiated by order_type:'warehouse'. Schema confirmed via Shadowfax's own Apiary 'Specification' example (their rendered docs never showed the Warehouse-model request body at all). Implemented as createShadowfaxWarehouseOrder() in src/shadowfax.js.
warehouseModelConfirmedLive_2026-07-24.firstRealLiveOrder: AWB SF3625058129DOF, placed 2026-07-24, pickup=Satya/Mullur(560035, unique_code='adc-test-pickup-01', made up — NOT pre-registered at the time), delivery=Jaya/Belathur-Kadugodi(560067), ₹60 Chocolate Chip Cookie, 100g. This is the very first genuinely-live (non-sandbox) Shadowfax order this project has ever created — real money was deducted from the Shadowfax wallet (100 → 53.98), confirming it's a real, billed shipment, not free/sandboxed.
warehouseModelConfirmedLive_2026-07-24.realBugFound_pickupDetailsBlank: The order-creation RESPONSE echoed pickup_details fully (name/contact/address all correct). But every SUBSEQUENT view — our own GET tracking call, AND the ShadowFax360 portal's 'Update Order' modal — shows pickup_details.name/contact/address_line_1 as EMPTY, while rto_details (sent with byte-identical data, since no separate rto was given) persisted and displays correctly, and customer_details (Jaya's) also displays correctly. Same input data, only pickup came back blank.
warehouseModelConfirmedLive_2026-07-24.workingTheory: pickup_details.unique_code is likely meant to reference a REAL pre-registered pickup address (via ShadowFax360's 'Manage Address' > 'Add Pickup Address' page), not an arbitrary self-chosen tag. We invented 'adc-test-pickup-01' without registering it anywhere, so the lookup likely found nothing and the address came back blank for storage/display purposes (even though creation's immediate response echoed what we submitted). rto_details, by contrast, doesn't appear to require a registered code — freeform text persisted fine. Not confirmed by Shadowfax directly — inferred from this evidence.
warehouseModelConfirmedLive_2026-07-24.updateAttemptResult: Tried fixing the existing stuck order via updateShadowfaxOrder() (POST /v3/clients/order_update/), setting pickup_details.contact/customer_address/pincode directly. REJECTED: HTTP 400, message 'Pickup details cannot be updated for this shipment'. This is a real, confirmed rejection from production — not a bug in our code. Likely because Warehouse-model pickup is meant to be fixed at creation (tied to a registered warehouse), not editable per-order like Marketplace's ad-hoc pickup_details can be (docs: 'pickup_details can be edited until the order is assigned_for_seller_pickup' — but this order is still status='new', so the rejection isn't about lifecycle stage, it's specific to the Warehouse model not allowing pickup edits at all).
warehouseModelConfirmedLive_2026-07-24.addressRegistered_2026-07-24: Registered a pickup address in ShadowFax360 > Manage Address: Label/Address Tag = 'ADC-Test-Pickup', Contact = Satya/9381502998, Address = 24-18-03, Green Fields Layout, Mullur, Bengaluru, Karnataka, 560035. The Manage Address LIST VIEW shows only this Label as an identifier — no separate system-generated ID/code visible anywhere in the UI. Working assumption: the Address Tag text itself ('ADC-Test-Pickup') IS the value to use as pickup_details.unique_code going forward — NOT YET CONFIRMED, since we haven't yet created an order referencing it to see if pickup_details populates correctly this time.
warehouseModelConfirmedLive_2026-07-24.planForTomorrow: (1) DONE 2026-07-24: cancelled SF3625058129DOF via cancelShadowfaxOrder() — succeeded, confirmed real (see Order Cancellation endpoint entry above). (2) NEXT: create ONE more real test order via createShadowfaxWarehouseOrder() with pickup.unique_code='ADC-Test-Pickup' (exact case matching the registered Address Tag) to confirm whether pickup_details now populates correctly. (3) If confirmed working, register the 4 REAL ADC stores (Jayanagar, S.G. Palya, Electronic City, Besant Nagar) the same way in ShadowFax360's Manage Address, each with its own Address Tag, and use those exact tags as unique_code for all real production orders going forward — this is the actual go-live blocker, not yet resolved as of this note.

shadowfax.statusLabelCrossCheck_2026-07-24: Tested every real official status_id from Shadowfax's Marketplace order-states table through sfxStatusLabel()/sfxStatusRank() directly (not guessed, actually ran it). Found and FIXED a real bug: 'picked' — Shadowfax's own official status_id for 'pickup completed' — was completely unrecognized (fell through to generic title-case, rank 0). Since sfxStatusRank() is what GET /api/orders/:id/track uses to decide whether a poll result should update the customer-visible shipment_status, rank 0 meant a genuine forward-progress update to 'picked' would have silently failed to register once Shadowfax actually started sending it (it never has yet — see below). Fixed: all 34 real official statuses now have a proper label; the 26 that form a clean, unambiguous linear forward path (new→…→delivered, plus the RTS/return sub-path) now have correct increasing ranks. 11 'exception' statuses (on_hold, pickup_on_hold, cid, seller_initiated_delay, seller_not_contactable, nc, na, pickup_not_attempted, reopen_ndr, pincode_updated, item_misrouted, cancelled_by_seller) were deliberately left unranked (rank 0) rather than guessed — they can interrupt the lifecycle at any point and there's no single correct place for them on a strictly-increasing scale; see the comment in shadowfax.js for the open question. Also confirmed: the webhook handler (routes/shadowfax.js) does NOT use rank at all — it stores whatever Shadowfax sends unconditionally (a push is never stale by definition), and DOES write estimated_delivery when the payload includes an eta — the earlier note that this column is 'never populated by any carrier' was accurate about what's happened so far but imprecise about the code: it's wired, just never received real data with that field. Separately: our webhook code recognizing 'seller_pickup_done' (from a real live webhook test earlier this project) is still real and still not in Shadowfax's own published docs at all — kept as an alias for 'picked', not removed.
shadowfax.stagingNeverProgressesPastNew_reconfirme: Re-polled all 3 real test AWBs (SF1784684342DOF, SF1788389792DOF, SF1789127573DOF) a full day+ after creation — every one still shows status=new, zero tracking events beyond the initial 'Item New at BLR_Domlur' scan, no rider assigned. This is now confirmed with fresh, repeated evidence (not a one-off) — staging genuinely does not self-progress orders through the pickup/delivery lifecycle. Awaiting Shadowfax's reply on whether that needs manual triggering on their end.

endpoints.purpose: Check if a pincode is serviceable for customer delivery
endpoints.liveTestedNote: The API call itself works and was confirmed live (I called it directly, not through any product code) — 'used: false' means our checkout flow never actually calls it, not that it's broken.
endpoints.ourEndpoint: NONE — sfxServiceability() is fully implemented in src/shadowfax.js but is DEAD CODE, never called from anywhere in the backend
endpoints.note_realResult: 560041 (our Jayanagar store) returns an EMPTY array — genuinely not serviceable on staging right now, confirmed live 2026-07-23, not a guess.
endpoints.realGapFound_2026-07-23: Checked src/routes/delivery.js /check route (what the checkout page calls to decide 'same-day Shadowfax' vs 'Pan-India Delhivery' and show the customer a delivery promise) — it ONLY does a local pincode-prefix match against the hardcoded SFX_STORES list (nearestStore()), it never calls this real serviceability API. Confirmed this caused a live mismatch: order ADC20260722210454 (id 68, dest pincode 560087) was told at checkout 'same-day from A Dough Cookie — Electronic City' via SHADOWFAX, but when the real shipment-creation call ran after payment, Shadowfax rejected all 3 in-zone pickup attempts with 'Invalid Delivery Pincode. Pincode 560087 is not serviceble' and the order silently fell back to Delhivery Pan-India instead. The order's OWN data ended up correct (order.carrier=DELHIVERY, and the customer's order confirmation correctly showed 'Pan-India · Delhivery' — verified from the user's own screenshot), so nothing is corrupted — but the checkout-time promise was wrong, and the switch to a slower carrier was never explicitly surfaced to the customer as a change. Wiring sfxServiceability() into /check would fix this at the source, but that's a product decision, not yet made.

endpoints.purpose: Create a forward (marketplace) intracity order — pickup from our store, deliver to customer
endpoints.ourEndpoint: internal — order auto-shipment flow, tries each in-zone store nearest-first
realCallsConfirmed_viaRailwayLogs: 2026-07-22T20:29:36Z — order ADC20260722202856 (id 64), dest=560100 → ✓ awb=SF1788389792DOF (pickup override 560007 used, real store pin 560100 — staging sandbox only services a handful of pickup pins, see SHADOWFAX_TEST_PICKUP_PIN note below)

2026-07-22T21:05:27Z — order ADC20260722210454 (id 68), dest=560087 → ✗ all 3 in-zone pickups rejected 'Invalid Delivery Pincode. Pincode 560087 is not serviceble', auto-fell-back to Delhivery (waybill 85582010000302) — see the dead-serviceability-check gap noted above
endpoints.sandboxNote: SHADOWFAX_TEST_PICKUP_PIN env var overrides our real store pincode (560100/560041/560029) with a staging-accepted one (560007) for the PICKUP side only — the destination pincode is always the real customer address. Must be left UNSET in production.

endpoints.purpose: Live tracking for an AWB
endpoints.liveTestedNote: Confirmed repeatedly (order 63, 64, and a fresh test order SF1789127573DOF created 2026-07-23) — always returns status=new, never further. See statusLabelCrossCheck note above re: why nothing has ever progressed past 'new' on staging (asked Shadowfax in the email sent 2026-07-23).
endpoints.ourEndpoint: GET /api/orders/:id/delhivery-track (branches to Shadowfax when carrier=SHADOWFAX)  and  GET /api/admin/orders/:id/track
endpoints.riderInfo: rider_name/rider_contact fields exist in this API's response shape (confirmed from the real order_details object) and get populated once status reaches 'ofd' (Out For Delivery) per Shadowfax's own docs — but since no real order has ever progressed past 'new' on staging, these fields have never actually been observed populated. Not fabricated, not assumed — genuinely untested because nothing has reached that state yet.
endpoints.customerTrackUrlRemovedFromAdminUI_2026-: The 'Live tracking link' the admin panel used to show (from customer_track_url) has been removed — it's been null in every real response we've ever seen, so we don't actually know what it links to. Not shown anywhere until confirmed. Admin still sees status + scan history, which is real.
endpoints.trackingApiMasksNameContact_2026-07-24: Confirmed: this endpoint returns name/contact/address_line_1/2 as EMPTY STRINGS for BOTH pickup_details AND delivery_details on every real order, regardless of registration status — only city/state/pincode come through. This is symmetric across pickup and delivery, so it's just privacy-masking on the tracking endpoint itself, NOT the same thing as the separate Pickup-Details-blank bug found in the ShadowFax360 portal's Update Order view (see shadowfax.warehouseModelConfirmedLive_2026-07-24 above) — that one is asymmetric (pickup blank, return/customer correct) and portal-specific.

endpoints.purpose: Track up to 50 AWBs in a single call
endpoints.ourEndpoint: bulkTrackShadowfax(awbs) in src/shadowfax.js — not yet wired to any route
endpoints.realCallConfirmed_2026-07-24: Tested live with our 2 real AWBs (SF3625058129DOF, SF36986267659) — succeeded on first try with request body {awb_numbers: [...]}. Confirmed SF3625058129DOF's final real status: cancelled_by_customer, remarks 'Cancelled as per client's request' — clean confirmation the earlier cancel-order test fully completed.

endpoints.purpose: Proof of delivery — recipient's signature sheet (S3 PDF) once delivered/rts_d
endpoints.liveTestedNote: Never actually called against a real AWB — no order has ever reached delivered/rts_d status on staging, so there's nothing real to fetch yet. Sample below is constructed from the documented response shape, not a real capture.

endpoints.purpose: Status update webhook — Shadowfax pushes rider/delivery status changes to us
endpoints.theirEndpoint: (inbound — Shadowfax calls US, configured in their dashboard)

endpoints.purpose: COD UPI-QR generator (closest thing to 'invoicing' Shadowfax offers — see invoiceGeneration note above; does NOT generate an invoice document, just a payment QR referencing one)
endpoints.theirEndpoint: POST https://saruman.staging.shadowfax.in/api/v2/clients/qr_code/generate/  (note: different subdomain — saruman, not dale — from every other Shadowfax endpoint we use)
endpoints.whyNotUsed: COD-only feature ('QR Code can only be generated for COD' per their own error message) — ADC is prepaid-only, so this endpoint can never apply. Deliberately staying unimplemented, per explicit instruction.

endpoints.purpose: Cancel an order by AWB — WANTED: admin-only control (normal users can never cancel a placed order — that stays strict)
endpoints.realCallConfirmed_2026-07-24: Cancelled the stuck test order SF3625058129DOF via cancelShadowfaxOrder() against real production — succeeded: {responseMsg: 'Request has been marked as cancelled', responseCode: 200}. First real confirmation this function works end-to-end.
endpoints.specConfirmed_2026-07-24: Real request/response schema pulled from Shadowfax's own Apiary 'Specification' tab via their Inspector tool (not their rendered docs, which never showed this) — genuinely their documented spec, not guessed.
endpoints.ourEndpoint: cancelShadowfaxOrder(awb, remarks) in src/shadowfax.js — not yet wired to an admin UI button

sampleResponse_specConfirmed.responseMsg: Request has been marked as cancelled

endpoints.gotcha: The field is `request_id`, NOT `awb_number`, even though the value you put there IS the AWB — easy to get wrong by symmetry with other endpoints.
endpoints.note: 200 = cancelled immediately; 304 = queued (order already past a certain in-flight stage, e.g. ofp/ofd/bag_in_transit); other codes = real failure (e.g. already delivered).

endpoints.purpose: Update order data (edit delivery_details/pickup_details/return_details, or transition status to rts/rto/reopen_ndr from on_hold/cancelled_by_customer only) — WANTED: admin-only control
endpoints.specConfirmed_2026-07-24: Real request/response schema pulled from Shadowfax's own Apiary 'Specification' tab via their Inspector tool.
endpoints.ourEndpoint: updateShadowfaxOrder(awb, updates) in src/shadowfax.js — not yet wired to an admin UI button
endpoints.gotcha: Field is `customer_address` inside delivery_details/pickup_details here — DIFFERENT from address_line_1/address_line_2 used at order-creation time. Only include the sections you're actually updating; per Shadowfax's docs, values inside an included section that you're not changing should be passed as null, not omitted.

endpoints.purpose: Escalation API — raise a support ticket for delayed delivery, expedite pickup, status mismatch, or delivery dispute — WANTED: admin-only control
endpoints.realCallConfirmed_2026-07-24: Raised a real escalation (issue_category=3, Expedite Pickup-Seller) on live order SF36986267659 via escalateShadowfaxIssue() — succeeded: {message: 'Issue received', status: 'SUCCESS'}.
endpoints.specConfirmed_2026-07-24: Real request/response schema pulled from Shadowfax's own Apiary 'Specification' tab via their Inspector tool.
endpoints.ourEndpoint: escalateShadowfaxIssue(awb, issueCategory) in src/shadowfax.js — not yet wired to an admin UI button
endpoints.note: issue_category: 1=Delayed Delivery, 2=Expedite Pickup-Customer, 3=Expedite Pickup-Seller, 4=Status Mismatch, 5=Delivery Dispute (per Shadowfax's docs).

endpoints.purpose: Printable shipping label
endpoints.whyNotUsed: Shadowfax has no printable label API — the rider physically collects from the store, so there's nothing to print (unlike Delhivery)

endpoints.purpose: Order/tax invoice
endpoints.whyNotUsed: See invoiceGeneration_answeredDirectly_2026-07-23 note at the top of this section — now confirmed against Shadowfax's complete official API reference, not a partial read: no invoice-generation endpoint exists anywhere in their API.

messageCentral.auth: authToken header — either a static dashboard token (AUTH_KEY) or generated on demand from CUSTOMER_ID + MC_PASSWORD

endpoints.purpose: Generate an auth token (only used if AUTH_KEY isn't set — we use the static-token path)
Send an OTP SMS
Validate the code the user typed
endpoints.theirEndpoint: GET /verification/v3/validateOtp?verificationId=…&code=…   (note: their docs say POST, but only GET actually works — documented as a gotcha in our own code comment)

endpoints.purpose: WhatsApp or Email OTP delivery channel (flowType alternatives to SMS)
endpoints.whyNotUsed: flowType is hardcoded to 'SMS' in sendOtp() — Message Central's VerifyNow supports other channels but we've never wired them in

endpoints.purpose: Voice OTP (call instead of SMS)
Custom branded sender ID / SMS template ("A Dough Cookie" in the message text)
endpoints.whyNotUsed: Investigated earlier this project: VerifyNow's SMS template is fixed/pre-approved globally, no per-customer branding via API. Only real options: ask Message Central support for a DLT-registered custom template, or migrate to their separate 'MessageNow' product (different API, own OTP generation/validation needed).

supabase.docs: https://supabase.com/docs/reference/javascript (via @supabase/supabase-js) — GoTrue Admin REST under the hood
supabase.auth: Service role key (admin client, backend-only) / anon key (frontend + anonClient signInWithPassword)

endpoints.purpose: Create a new auth user (new phone-OTP signup)
Update an existing auth user (reset one-time password each OTP login, sync display name/phone)
Delete an auth user (cleanup after merging a duplicate phone/email account into one)
endpoints.ourEndpoint: internal — mergeAccounts() in auth.js and middleware.js

endpoints.purpose: Exchange email+password for a real session (mint tokens after OTP verify)
Google OAuth sign-in (entirely client-side, no backend endpoint at all)
endpoints.note: Backend only gets involved AFTER login, via parseAuth/syncUser reading the resulting JWT to create/sync the users row and assign role from ADMIN_EMAILS/ADMIN_PHONES

endpoints.purpose: Direct SQL read of the auth.users table (NOT the SDK) — used to look up an existing Supabase user by synthetic email/phone before deciding create vs update
endpoints.theirEndpoint: raw Postgres: SELECT id, raw_user_meta_data->>'full_name' AS full_name FROM auth.users WHERE email = $1 OR phone = $2
endpoints.note: Bypasses the GoTrue REST API entirely — direct DB access to Supabase's own auth schema via our existing Postgres connection. Best-effort (wrapped in try/catch since we may not always have schema access).

endpoints.purpose: List all users / generate magic links / manage MFA factors
endpoints.whyNotUsed: Never needed — our own users table + role column is the source of truth for admin listing

mailer.provider: Resend (HTTPS API) — switched from Zoho SMTP on 2026-07-22
mailer.whySwitchedFromSmtp: Railway's Hobby plan blocks ALL outbound SMTP (ports 25/465/587) — every send via Zoho SMTP was silently failing with a 10s connection timeout (confirmed: 100% failure rate across every real order placed, e.g. ADC20260722202856, ADC20260722203817, ADC20260722203833 — Railway support KB confirms SMTP is Pro-plan+ only). Resend's API is plain HTTPS, which Railway doesn't restrict. Old SMTP/nodemailer implementation kept commented out at the bottom of mailer.js (not deleted) as the fallback if this project ever moves to a Railway plan that allows SMTP again.
mailer.domainVerification: adoughcookie.com verified in Resend via a dedicated 'send.' subdomain (MX + SPF + DKIM records scoped to send.adoughcookie.com) — deliberately does not touch the root domain's existing Zoho MX/SPF/DKIM, so Zoho Mail's inbox/webmail keeps working untouched. Emails still send FROM info@adoughcookie.com despite the verification living on the subdomain (Resend/SES support this pattern).

endpoints.purpose: Send an email (used for both contact-form notifications and order confirmations)
endpoints.ourEndpoint: POST /api/contact  (business notification)  and  internal finalizePaidOrder() → sendOrderEmails() (order confirmation + business copy)
endpoints.note: Live-captured 2026-07-22 21:15:58 UTC — both the customer email (id 7a05d482…) and the business copy to info@adoughcookie.com (id b33cbe25…) sent successfully for real order ADC20260722211553. The immediately-prior order (ADC20260722210454, 21:04) failed with 'The adoughcookie.com domain is not verified' — domain verification finished in the ~11 minutes between the two, so this is a genuine before/after comparison, not a guess.

endpoints.purpose: Best-effort city/region lookup for wherever a login came from (fills users.last_login_location, once per fresh session)
endpoints.note: Never throws — a failed lookup just leaves the column unchanged; never blocks or delays login

realFinding_liveCaptured_2026-07-22.xffParsedCorrectly: user=7 | xff="13.234.202.9, 152.233.68.97" | resolved_ip=13.234.202.9 — leftmost (real client) IP correctly extracted from the X-Forwarded-For chain, not Railway's proxy IP
realFinding_liveCaptured_2026-07-22.butIpapiRateLimited: user=7 | ✗ ipapi.co status=429 | {"error":true,"reason":"RateLimited","message":"Visit https://ipapi.co/ratelimited/ for details"} — the free tier got rate-limited on a real request, so last_login_location stayed unset for this login
realFinding_liveCaptured_2026-07-22.implication: The XFF-parsing fix itself works correctly; the remaining gap is ipapi.co's free-tier rate limit, which is a real, observed failure mode (not hypothetical) — a busier login volume will hit this regularly. Not fixed yet — flagging for a decision (paid tier, a different free geolocation API, or accept the gaps) rather than silently living with it.
